video_title: Stranger Things 5 | One Last Adventure | Netflix
comment_text:
These "higher stakes" everyone's mentioning is a characteristic which makes the later seasons worse. In S1/2 I could totally believe that a group of young friends gets involuntary dragged into the strange occurrences happening in Hawkings. In later seasons, however, the story gets too grand and it doesn't feel plausible that these kids are still are a part of it. Infiltrating a Russian base is for example totally unbelievable and ruins the atmosphere/story. 

It still works alright though, but IMO S1/2 is ten out of ten, and later seasons drop that score.

comment_text: I started watching it the day it premiered!!! I remember being enthralled and left wanting more after the last episode of the first Season. I introduced it to my grandkids!!! It started the tradition of waiting for the next Season and having an epic watch night of each Season. Just as I have watched the kids grow up, my own grandkids have grown up as well and this last Season will be bittersweet. A real goodbye to their childhood and my memories of the excited looks on their faces watching the episodes!!! Goodbye Will, Eleven, Mike, Lucas, Dustin! Thanks for the ride ❤️❤️❤️❤️

comment_text: Stranger Things, for me, is a masterclass on how to write a story. It's a masterpiece from season 1 to season 4, and soon hopefully (I have actually no doubt) season 5.  This is how you write a good story, a story about the CHARACTERS, not about a political agenda or diversity checklist! And where every gender is equally awesome characters! If you are about to like my comment just because I said diversity checklist, GO AWAY. I don't mind diversity at all! WHEN IT MAKES SENSE, and it's not forced. When the character you wrote feels from the start like a transgender or anything else, I'm on board. If you are not... Well think about how these people never ASKED to be like that, they were born like that. If you don't understand that you're part of the problem.

video_title: Stranger Things 5 | One Last Adventure | Netflix
comment_text: Best show on netflix and for me personally the best show EVER. I love each and every one of these actors and their characters. Im 54 in 1982 I was the same age as these kids are in the show. I can tell you now that era was captured brilliantly. We all rode around on put BMXs and Chopper bikes. We made camps. We all literally used our imaginations to entertain ourselves. We laughed so much. For me this show represents my magical childhood. I was also bullied by some kids but my friends also stuck up for me. My mum and dad where divorced which was rare. Thats why I was bullied because we where poor. But even though we where poor we where so happy because our love family friends and community meant so much more then material things. Thank Duffer Brothers for the best show ever. Thank for literally capturing my childhood. This last part is so so sad but we are also so so excited. Please never stop making shows you two are super talented and the best. Thank you ❤
